title: Round() with GROUP BY yield wrong result
description:
### What happens?

Using GROUP BY command on Round() produces wrong results.

### To Reproduce
Execute those SQL in duckdb
```
create table zz(id smallint primary key, si smallint);


INSERT INTO zz (id, si)
SELECT 
    CAST(generate_series AS SMALLINT),
    CAST(generate_series AS SMALLINT)
FROM generate_series(1, 1000);


select ROUND( 53, si )as ag_column3 from zz GROUP BY ag_column3 order by ag_column3;
```

should produce 53, but produce 
```
│ 52.99999999999999 │
│              53.0 │
│ 53.00000000000001 │
```

### OS:

centos

### DuckDB Version:

compiled from current main branch

### DuckDB Client:

command line

### Hardware:

_No response_

### Full Name:

bobi liu

### Affiliation:

-

- [x] Yes, I have

- [ ] Yes, I have
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_table(id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, value FLOAT, category VARCHAR(50));
INSERT INTO test_table (id, value, category) VALUES (1, 52.5, 'A'), (2, 53.5, 'B'), (3, 54.5, 'C');
SELECT ROUND(value) AS rounded_value FROM test_table GROUP BY rounded_value ORDER BY rounded_value;
CREATE TABLE boundary_test(id SMALLINT, num_value DOUBLE);
key_bug_features: GROUP BY with ROUND() produces incorrect rounding results
